description: 78F with HTN and T2DM. Age >= 76 triggers Grade I (insufficient evidence) for statin primary prevention.

actions.label: Acknowledge insufficient evidence for statin initiation in adults >= 76
actions.rationale: USPSTF Grade I: current evidence is insufficient to assess the balance of benefits and harms of initiating a statin for primary prevention in adults 76 and older.

actions.label: Continue current medications (losartan, metformin)
actions.rationale: Patient is on losartan for HTN and metformin for T2DM. No indication to change; these manage existing conditions.

actions.label: Individualized discussion about cardiovascular risk management
actions.rationale: Given age >= 76 and multiple CVD risk factors (HTN, T2DM, former smoker), clinician should discuss risk/benefit of statin on individual basis, acknowledging the evidence gap.

contraindications.label: Routine statin initiation for primary prevention
contraindications.rationale: Grade I means insufficient evidence — should not be interpreted as a positive recommendation to initiate.
